_meta.purpose: Per-state business formation and annual compliance costs, for the 'where should I form my business?' comparison calculator. One row per jurisdiction, separate figures for LLC and corporation because they differ in almost every state.
_meta.status: SKELETON — AWAITING OWNER SIGN-OFF. Not wired to any page or engine.
_meta.rule: No figure enters this file without a source URL. Filing fees change yearly and are exactly what the never-invent rule in CLAUDE.md covers. A null is a correct and expected value; a guessed number is not.

<STATE>.llc / <STATE>.corp.formationFee: number|null — one-time Secretary of State fee to file Articles of Organization (LLC) or Incorporation (corp)
<STATE>.llc / <STATE>.corp.foreignQualificationFee: number|null — one-time fee for an entity formed elsewhere to register to do business in this state. THIS IS THE HONESTY ROW: it is what an out-of-state formation actually costs on top.

annualReport.dueDate: string|null — plain English, e.g. 'first day of the anniversary month'
annualReport.note: string|null — say so here if the fee varies by capital, shares or revenue rather than being flat

annualTax.amount: number|null — a mandatory annual tax DISTINCT from the report fee, e.g. Delaware LLC annual tax, California minimum franchise tax

<STATE>.llc / <STATE>.corp.source: string|null — URL on a state government domain. Incorporation-service sites are not acceptable sources: they bundle their own fees.
<STATE>.llc / <STATE>.corp.feeVaries: boolean|null — TRUE where the fee is not flat but scales with authorized shares, capital, par value or revenue. The stored number is then the MINIMUM, not the price, and the engine must render it as 'from $X' and flag the column unquantified. Storing a minimum as if it were a fee is the quietest way this dataset could lie.
<STATE>.llc / <STATE>.corp.feeVariesNote: string|null — what it scales with, in plain words.

<STATE>.grossReceiptsTax.rate: number|null — null where the regime exists but is not reducible to one rate. The engine must then compute null and surface the note, never a guess.
<STATE>.grossReceiptsTax.threshold: number|null — below this, the tax is zero

<STATE>.franchiseTax.structured: object|null — parameters where real arithmetic is possible (Delaware's two methods, California's minimum, Texas's margin rate). null where only prose exists.

notModelled: Registered agent cost — a MARKET PRICE, not government data. Modeled as a user-selectable tier in the island and labelled 'typical market range', never presented as an official figure.
Sales tax and economic nexus — no dataset on this site; the calculator names the states and says explicitly that it does not compute this.
Local business licenses, city and county registration
Federal beneficial ownership (Corporate Transparency Act) filing costs, unless the owner adds verified figures
Fee changes across the five-year window — the projection holds today's fees flat and says so
_meta.legacyHarvest: Figures tagged source 'legacy/js/app.js <view>' came from the previous site and are UNVERIFIED against a government source. They are seeded here so the owner can re-verify rather than re-find them. Treat them as leads, not as data.
_meta.url: /business-formation-state-calculator/  (owner decision; /where-to-form-llc/ was the alternate and was not chosen)

registeredAgentTiers._note: Owner decision: use the legacy tiers. These are MARKET PRICES, not government fees. Copy must label them 'typical market range' and never present them as an official figure. Self-agent is $0 but is only selectable for a state where the owner actually has an address — the engine enforces that on home and work state columns only.
registeredAgentTiers.source: legacy/js/app.js delawareFormationView (market tiers, not a state schedule)

annualReport.note: No Secretary of State annual report; requirement repealed.

llc.researchNote: No annual report. Name reservation certificate required BEFORE formation, an extra $25-28 not included in the formation fee.

annualReport.note: No SOS annual report. Separate Alabama Business Privilege Tax with Dept of Revenue; fully exempt when computed tax is $100 or less for years beginning after 12/31/2023.

corp.researchNote: No annual report. Name reservation certificate required BEFORE formation, an extra $25-28 not included in the formation fee.

annualReport.note: Biennial $100 domestic; $200 foreign. Free initial report after formation.

llc.researchNote: Report is BIENNIAL not annual, and DOUBLES to $200 for foreign entities — the five-year projection must halve the frequency and use the foreign figure for out-of-state columns.

annualReport.note: Biennial $100 domestic; $200 foreign.

corp.researchNote: Report is BIENNIAL not annual, and DOUBLES to $200 for foreign entities — the five-year projection must halve the frequency and use the foreign figure for out-of-state columns.

annualReport.note: Arizona LLCs file no annual report; corporations only.

llc.researchNote: LLC schedule revised 3/2026; corporation schedule revised 2023 and is the latest published. Arizona LLCs also have a newspaper publication requirement outside Maricopa and Pima counties.

annualReport.note: For-profit corporation $45 regular, $80 expedited.

corp.researchNote: LLC schedule revised 3/2026; corporation schedule revised 2023 and is the latest published. Arizona LLCs also have a newspaper publication requirement outside Maricopa and Pima counties.

annualReport.note: No separate report; flat $150 annual franchise tax report filed with SOS, plus $5 online processing.

llc.researchNote: Fees shown are ONLINE rates; paper is $50 formation and $300 foreign. Franchise tax replaces the annual report.

annualReport.note: Stock corporation franchise tax minimum $150, scaling with outstanding capital stock; non-stock $300.

corp.researchNote: Fees shown are ONLINE rates; paper is $50 formation and $300 foreign. Franchise tax replaces the annual report.
corp.feeVariesNote: Stock corporation franchise tax scales with outstanding capital stock above a $150 minimum. The stored figure is the minimum.

annualReport.dueDate: within 90 days of registration, then biennially by the end of the anniversary month
annualReport.note: Statement of Information LLC-12, $20. FTB collects a $250 penalty for failure to file.

annualTax.note: $800 annual LLC tax, due the 15th day of the 4th month, payable even with no activity until the LLC is cancelled. THE FIRST-YEAR EXEMPTION IS DEAD FOR 2026 — it covered only tax years 2021 to 2023.

llc.researchNote: THE MYTH-WARNING FACT: an out-of-state LLC doing business in California owes the $800 whether or not it registers with the Secretary of State. FTB treats you as doing business if you transact for financial gain in California, are commercially domiciled there, or exceed the 2025 thresholds — California sales $757,070, property $75,707, payroll $75,707, or 25% of total.

annualReport.dueDate: within 90 days of incorporation, then annually
annualReport.note: Statement of Information SI-550: $20 filing plus $5 disclosure.

annualTax.note: $800 minimum franchise tax for every corporation incorporated, registered OR DOING BUSINESS in California. Newly incorporated corporations are exempt in their first taxable year and that exemption IS still live in 2026 — unlike the LLC one.

corp.researchNote: THE MYTH-WARNING FACT: an out-of-state LLC doing business in California owes the $800 whether or not it registers with the Secretary of State. FTB treats you as doing business if you transact for financial gain in California, are commercially domiciled there, or exceed the 2025 thresholds — California sales $757,070, property $75,707, payroll $75,707, or 25% of total.

grossReceiptsTax.note: LLC fee on total California income, SEPARATE FROM AND IN ADDITION TO the $800: $0 under $250,000; $900 to $499,999; $2,500 to $999,999; $6,000 to $4,999,999; $11,790 at $5,000,000 or more. Tiered, so no single rate.

annualReport.note: Periodic Report $25 online; $50 late penalty.

llc.researchNote: All formation filings are online only. Foreign qualification is the Foreign Entity Authority Statement, $100 for both entity types.

annualReport.note: Periodic Report $25 online; $50 late penalty.

corp.researchNote: All formation filings are online only. Foreign qualification is the Foreign Entity Authority Statement, $100 for both entity types.

annualReport.note: Filed online 1 Jan to March 31; $80 domestic and foreign alike.

llc.researchNote: Domestic stock corporations also owe a separate $150 Organization and First Report. The foreign qualification fee of $385 includes a $285 license fee. Corporation annual report differs sharply: $150 domestic against $435 foreign.

annualReport.note: Domestic stock corporation $150; FOREIGN stock corporation annual report is $435.

corp.researchNote: Domestic stock corporations also owe a separate $150 Organization and First Report. The foreign qualification fee of $385 includes a $285 license fee. Corporation annual report differs sharply: $150 domestic against $435 foreign.

annualReport.note: Delaware does not require LLCs, LPs or GPs to file an annual report.

annualTax.note: Flat $300 alternative-entity tax due on or before June 1, domestic and foreign alike, no proration. Late: $200 penalty plus 1.5% interest a month.

llc.researchNote: CORP FORMATION IS A MINIMUM — the schedule marks it VARIES BASED ON STOCK. Column-one amount covers filing, indexing, data entry, municipality and one page of county recording; $9 per extra page. The LLC $300 is a TAX, not a report.

annualReport.note: $50 non-exempt domestic, $25 exempt. FOREIGN corporations file a DIFFERENT report: $125 due June 30 with a $125 late penalty.

annualTax.note: Franchise tax computed separately, paid with the March 1 report. Minimum $175 authorized-shares method or $400 assumed par value method.

corp.researchNote: CORP FORMATION IS A MINIMUM — the schedule marks it VARIES BASED ON STOCK. Column-one amount covers filing, indexing, data entry, municipality and one page of county recording; $9 per extra page. The LLC $300 is a TAX, not a report.
corp.feeVariesNote: Delaware incorporation fee is marked "VARIES BASED ON STOCK" on the official schedule. The stored $109 is the minimum, and $9 is added per extra page of county recording.

structured.largeCorporateFilerDefinition: Listed on a national exchange AND consolidated annual gross revenues or assets of $750,000,000 or more.

franchiseTax.note: Two methods. Delaware BILLS the authorized-shares method by default; the assumed par value method is usually far cheaper for a company with many authorized shares and modest assets, and must be elected.

annualReport.note: BRA-25 biennial report $300 plus $100 late fee. First due April 1 of the year after registration.

llc.researchNote: Corp formation $99 covers authorized capital to $100,000, rising to $550, $1,100 and $1,650. SEPARATE mandatory DC franchise tax: D-20 and D-30 both carry a $250 minimum, $1,000 above $1M of DC gross receipts.

annualReport.note: BRA-25 $300 biennially.

corp.researchNote: Corp formation $99 covers authorized capital to $100,000, rising to $550, $1,100 and $1,650. SEPARATE mandatory DC franchise tax: D-20 and D-30 both carry a $250 minimum, $1,000 above $1M of DC gross receipts.
corp.feeVariesNote: DC incorporation is $99 for authorized capital to $100,000, then $550, $1,100 and $1,650 by tier. The stored figure is the minimum.

annualReport.note: $538.75 if received after May 1 — a $400 late fee. Administrative dissolution the fourth Friday of September.

annualTax.note: No entity-level annual tax; an LLC not electing corporate treatment is outside Florida corporate income tax.

llc.researchNote: FORMATION FIGURES ARE REQUIRED COMPONENTS: LLC $100 filing plus $25 agent designation; corp $35 plus $35. Sunbiz's headline $160 / $87.50 totals BUNDLE OPTIONAL extras (certified copy, certificate of status) — copying those totals overstates the cost.

annualReport.note: $550 if received after May 1.

annualTax.note: No flat annual tax; corporate income tax 5.5% of Florida net income after a $50,000 exemption.

corp.researchNote: FORMATION FIGURES ARE REQUIRED COMPONENTS: LLC $100 filing plus $25 agent designation; corp $35 plus $35. Sunbiz's headline $160 / $87.50 totals BUNDLE OPTIONAL extras (certified copy, certificate of status) — copying those totals overstates the cost.

grossReceiptsTax.note: No general gross receipts tax. Corporate income tax 5.5% on net income after a $50,000 exemption; S corporations paying federal tax on Form 1120S line 23c are subject to it.

llc.researchNote: SOURCING CAVEAT: nvsos.gov blocks automated fetch, so every figure comes from the NEVADA STATUTES on leg.state.nv.us rather than the SOS fee-schedule PDF. THE BUSINESS LICENCE IS THE BURIED COST: $200 LLC / $500 corp EVERY YEAR on top of the $150 list. Corp formation and list fees are share-value dependent; $75/$150 are minimums.

annualReport.dueDate: last day of the anniversary month
annualReport.note: Annual List of Officers and Directors. $150 only if total authorized stock value is $75,000 or less, then rising to a maximum of $11,125. Initial List is flat $150.

annualTax.note: State Business License, $500 a year for corporations (NRS 76.100, 76.130).

corp.researchNote: SOURCING CAVEAT: nvsos.gov blocks automated fetch, so every figure comes from the NEVADA STATUTES on leg.state.nv.us rather than the SOS fee-schedule PDF. THE BUSINESS LICENCE IS THE BURIED COST: $200 LLC / $500 corp EVERY YEAR on top of the $150 list. Corp formation and list fees are share-value dependent; $75/$150 are minimums.
corp.feeVariesNote: Nevada corporation formation and annual list fees both scale with the value of total authorized shares — formation from $75 to a $35,000 maximum, annual list from $150 to $11,125. Both stored figures are minimums.

grossReceiptsTax.note: Commerce Tax on Nevada gross revenue above $4,000,000 per fiscal year. Businesses at or below the threshold are exempt from filing. The RATE VARIES BY NAICS CATEGORY, so no single rate is stored and the engine must compute null above the threshold.

annualReport.note: Ohio requires NO annual or biennial report for LLCs. The statutory schedule lists periodic reports only for LLPs and professional associations.

llc.researchNote: CONFLICT FLAGGED: ORC 111.16 (effective April 3, 2025) sets $0.10 per share with a $99 minimum, and SOS Form 532A charges $99, but the Legislative Service Commission fee table dated January 30, 2026 still prints a $125 minimum. $99 used. Ohio's no-annual-report position is verified negatively — the statutory schedule lists none — because ohiosos.gov serves a JavaScript-only shell.

annualReport.note: No annual report for domestic or foreign for-profit corporations.

corp.researchNote: CONFLICT FLAGGED: ORC 111.16 (effective April 3, 2025) sets $0.10 per share with a $99 minimum, and SOS Form 532A charges $99, but the Legislative Service Commission fee table dated January 30, 2026 still prints a $125 minimum. $99 used. Ohio's no-annual-report position is verified negatively — the statutory schedule lists none — because ohiosos.gov serves a JavaScript-only shell.
corp.feeVariesNote: Ohio incorporation is $0.10 per authorized share under ORC 111.16 with a floor, so it rises above the stored figure once authorized shares pass roughly 1,250. The stored figure is a minimum.
corp.ownerDecision: OWNER DECISION: $125, from the Legislative Service Commission fee table dated January 30, 2026. The other figure is $99, from ORC 111.16 (effective April 3, 2025, $0.10 per share with a $99 floor) and SOS Form 532A. The state disagrees with itself; the owner chose the higher, later-dated table. This is the conservative direction — if the SOS in fact charges $99, the calculator overstates Ohio corporation formation by $26, which is the safer way to be wrong on a one-time fee.

grossReceiptsTax.note: Commercial Activity Tax. From tax year 2025: $6,000,000 exclusion, 0.26% above it. Registration required within 30 days of exceeding $6 million.

annualReport.note: VARIES: $50 per member, $300 minimum, $3,000 maximum.

llc.researchNote: LLC formation and foreign qualification are BOTH per-member: $50 a member, $300 minimum, $3,000 maximum. Corp fees flat. Separate franchise and excise tax with a $100 minimum.
llc.feeVariesNote: LLC formation and foreign qualification are $50 per member, minimum $300, maximum $3,000. The stored figure is the minimum.

annualReport.note: Flat $20; extra $20 if the agent is changed on the report.

corp.researchNote: LLC formation and foreign qualification are BOTH per-member: $50 a member, $300 minimum, $3,000 maximum. Corp fees flat. Separate franchise and excise tax with a $100 minimum.

annualReport.note: No SOS annual report. Comptroller requires an annual Public Information Report with the franchise report; no filing fee.

annualTax.note: Franchise (margin) tax, no flat minimum. $0 due at or below $2,650,000 annualized revenue for report year 2026, but the report must still be filed.

llc.researchNote: No personal income tax. The $750 foreign registration is among the highest in the country and is exactly the cost the myth warning exists to surface.

annualReport.note: No SOS annual report; Public Information Report to the Comptroller, no fee.

corp.researchNote: No personal income tax. The $750 foreign registration is among the highest in the country and is exactly the cost the myth warning exists to surface.

grossReceiptsTax.note: Report year 2026: no-tax-due threshold $2,650,000. Standard 0.75%, retail/wholesale 0.375%, EZ computation 0.331% under $20M revenue. A report is required even below the threshold.

annualReport.note: Annual renewal $18 including a $5 portal surcharge; $10 late fee.

llc.researchNote: Schedule is headed Fiscal Year 2026, effective July 1, 2025. No FY2027 schedule found on the .gov site.

annualReport.note: Annual renewal, same figures.

corp.researchNote: Schedule is headed Fiscal Year 2026, effective July 1, 2025. No FY2027 schedule found on the .gov site.

annualReport.note: Domestic LLC $45; FOREIGN LLC annual report is $170.

llc.researchNote: Formation and foreign qualification $155 for both types, but the ANNUAL report is far higher for foreign entities — $170 LLC and $250 corp against $45 and $60 domestic. Minimum corporate income tax from $100.

annualReport.note: Domestic corporation $60; FOREIGN corporation annual report is $250.

corp.researchNote: Formation and foreign qualification $155 for both types, but the ANNUAL report is far higher for foreign entities — $170 LLC and $250 corp against $45 and $60 domestic. Minimum corporate income tax from $100.

annualReport.note: Flat $50 annual registration fee; no report content. $25 late penalty.

llc.researchNote: Stock corporation charter fee VARIES: $25 filing plus $50 per 25,000 authorized shares, $75 minimum, $2,525 maximum. Same schedule for the foreign entrance fee.

annualReport.note: VARIES by authorized shares: $100 for 1-5,000, rising $30 per additional 5,000. Nonstock $25.

corp.researchNote: Stock corporation charter fee VARIES: $25 filing plus $50 per 25,000 authorized shares, $75 minimum, $2,525 maximum. Same schedule for the foreign entrance fee.
corp.feeVariesNote: Stock corporation charter fee is $25 plus $50 per 25,000 authorized shares, minimum $75, maximum $2,525. The annual registration fee also scales. Stored figures are minimums.

annualReport.note: Initial report within 120 days: free with formation, otherwise $10. Annual report $70, $95 delinquent.

llc.researchNote: No corporate income tax, but B&O is on gross receipts with no cost deduction — a loss-making business still owes it.

annualReport.note: Same as LLC.

corp.researchNote: No corporate income tax, but B&O is on gross receipts with no cost deduction — a loss-making business still owes it.

grossReceiptsTax.note: Business & Occupation tax on GROSS receipts with NO deduction for costs. Rates vary across more than 50 classifications, so no single rate is stored and the engine must compute null.

annualReport.note: Due 1 Jan to June 30; $50 late fee for profit entities.

llc.researchNote: Business Registration Certificate is $30 per location, not the $100 often quoted. $1 portal fee on online filings.

annualReport.note: Same window and late fee.

corp.researchNote: Business Registration Certificate is $30 per location, not the $100 often quoted. $1 portal fee on online filings.

annualReport.note: Domestic LLC $25; FOREIGN LLC $80. $15 paper surcharge since March 2024.

llc.researchNote: The domestic LLC annual report figure ($25 against $40) could not be read cleanly off the DFI table and needs a human check. Corp $40 and foreign $80 are confirmed.
llc.ownerDecision: OWNER DECISION: $25 for the domestic LLC annual report, resolving the $25-or-$40 ambiguity on the DFI table. The foreign LLC figure of $80 is separately confirmed and unaffected.

annualReport.note: Domestic stock corporation $40; foreign corporation $80.

corp.researchNote: The domestic LLC annual report figure ($25 against $40) could not be read cleanly off the DFI table and needs a human check. Corp $40 and foreign $80 are confirmed.

annualReport.dueDate: first day of the anniversary month
annualReport.note: License tax: greater of $60 or $0.0002 per dollar of assets located and employed in Wyoming. May be filed up to 120 days early.

annualTax.note: No separate annual tax. Wyoming has no corporate or personal income tax.

llc.researchNote: CORRECTS the legacy figure of $102 — the current schedule is $100. Rate applies to WYOMING-SITUS assets only, not worldwide. The SOS FAQ writes it loosely as '.0002%' but its own worked example ($1,210,000 assets = $242) confirms the multiplier is 0.0002. Series LLCs add $10 per series.

annualReport.dueDate: first day of the anniversary month
annualReport.note: Same license tax formula. Figure is for profit corporations; nonprofits pay a flat $25.

corp.researchNote: CORRECTS the legacy figure of $102 — the current schedule is $100. Rate applies to WYOMING-SITUS assets only, not worldwide. The SOS FAQ writes it loosely as '.0002%' but its own worked example ($1,210,000 assets = $242) confirms the multiplier is 0.0002. Series LLCs add $10 per series.

annualReportLicenseTax.formula: greater of $60 or $0.0002 x assets located and employed in Wyoming
